response.justification: While the candidate exceeds the experience requirement with 19 years in software engineering and holds a relevant CS degree from ETH Zürich, the core technical fit is poor. C++ experience is limited to 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015), now 10 years outdated. The resume shows a career trajectory focused on full-stack web development (Node.js, TypeScript, React) rather than systems programming, multi-threading, or embedded/industrial automation. Critically absent are any demonstrated expertise in industrial automation, IoT, cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443 SDL), message brokers, or protocols like MQTT and Modbus. The candidate would require substantial ramp-up in core technical competencies for this role.
strengths: Extensive software engineering background (19 years) with proven ability to architect distributed, event-driven systems
Advanced full-stack capabilities including Node.js, TypeScript, and React, plus experience with CI/CD pipelines and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, pairing)
Relevant degree (BS Computer Science, ETH Zürich) and multiple senior-level roles demonstrating architectural thinking and team collaboration
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and severely outdated (6 months in 2014–2015); resume shows 10-year shift away from systems programming toward web technologies
No demonstrated expertise in industrial automation, IoT, cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), or industrial protocols (Modbus, MQTT, IEC 61850); these are core job requirements
Career trajectory and skill set indicate a web/full-stack engineer, not a systems programmer experienced with multi-threading, concurrent programming, or embedded real-time development

key_factors.factor: C++ systems programming expertise and recency
Industrial automation and cybersecurity standards knowledge
Architectural design thinking and seniority level
